key_insight: The paper introduces a fully matrix-free, differentiable PyTorch phase-field fracture solver that runs on CPUs/GPUs without custom compiled code and supports inverse analysis through autograd.
engineering_relevance: It enables scalable fracture simulation and parameter identification in a single open-source workflow, making physics-based damage modeling more accessible for research and ML-integrated engineering pipelines.
method: Element-wise finite-element mechanics with scatter-based accumulation, avoiding global sparse stiffness assembly in explicit dynamics.
Support for AT1/AT2 phase-field models, multiple energy splits, and plane stress/strain options.
Custom backward rule for the implicit bound-constrained damage solve using implicit differentiation through CG.
evidence_signal: The solver was benchmarked on four dynamic and two quasi-static fracture cases, and recovered fracture energy $G_c$ with relative error below 10^-3 in only a few L-BFGS states.
watch_item: Performance and robustness may depend on CG convergence and on the cost/accuracy tradeoff of implicit differentiation for the damage solve in large-scale problems.
paper_title: A matrix-free, differentiable PyTorch solver for phase-field fracture: Formulation, benchmarks, and inverse analysis
